LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS KATHLEEN ORTIZ, JOANNA ORTIZ'S MOTION FOR PAGA SETTLEMENT APPROVAL. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE OVERALL AMOUNT OF THE PROPOSED SETTLEMENT IS FAIR AND ADEQUATE IN VIEW OF THE PURPOSES AND POLICIES OF PAGA, TAKING INTO ACCOUNT SUCH FACTORS AS THE ADEQUACY OF THE CIVIL PENALTIES IN LIGHT OF THE POTENTIAL MAXIMUM IF THE MATTER WERE TO GO TO VERDICT AND THE RISKS OF LITIGATION. (LAB. CODE 2699(L)(2).) THE COURT FURTHER FINDS THAT THE AMOUNT OF ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS SOUGHT IS REASONABLE. (LAB. CODE 2699(G)(1).) ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR# 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-01-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Paga Settlement Approval; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar May-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Terry E. Sanchez, Esq., Munger, tolles & Olsen LLP, Telephoen 213-683-9179, appearing for Defendant Terry E. Sanchez.
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS KATHLEEN ORTIZ, JOANNA ORTIZ'S Motion For PAGA Settlement Approval.

Plaintiffs Kathleen Ortiz and Joanna Ortiz's motion for PAGA settlement approval is granted. The Court finds that the overall amount of the proposed settlement is fair and adequate in view of the purposes and policies of PAGA, taking into account such factors as the adequacy of the civil penalties in light of the potential maximum if the matter were to go to verdict and the risks of litigation. (Lab. Code 2699(l)(2).) The Court further finds that the amount of attorney's fees and costs sought is reasonable. (Lab. Code 2699(g)(1).)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
